name: Temporal Bone CT
description: High-resolution CT of the temporal bone for evaluation of middle ear, inner ear, and mastoid pathology

findings.technique: High-resolution CT of the temporal bones was performed in the axial and coronal planes with bone algorithm reconstructions.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: External Auditory Canal
sections.content: The bilateral external auditory canals are [patent / no stenosis / no erosion]. No foreign body or debris.

sections.name: Middle Ear Cavity
sections.content: The bilateral middle ear cavities are [aerated / no fluid / no mass]. The ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes) are [intact / normal].

sections.name: Mastoid Air Cells
sections.content: The bilateral mastoid air cells are [well-pneumatized / no opacification / no coalescence]. No mastoiditis.

sections.name: Cochlea
sections.content: The bilateral cochleae demonstrate [normal bony architecture / no deformity / no ossification]. The cochlear turns are [patent / normal].

sections.name: Vestibular Apparatus
sections.content: The bilateral semicircular canals are [intact / normal]. The vestibule is [normal / no abnormal calcification].

sections.name: Internal Auditory Canals
sections.content: The bilateral internal auditory canals are [patent / adequate in diameter]. No mass or lesion in the canals.

sections.name: Facial Nerve
sections.content: The bilateral facial nerve canals are [intact / no dehiscence / no abnormal course]. The geniculate ganglion is [normal / unremarkable].

sections.name: ossicles
sections.content: The ossicular chain is [intact / no discontinuity / no erosion]. The stapes footplate is [mobile / not fixated].

sections.name: Eustachian Tubes
sections.content: The bilateral eustachian tubes are [patent / open]. No obstruction identified.

sections.name: Petroquous Apex
sections.content: The bilateral petrous apices demonstrate [no abnormality / no erosion / no cystic change]. No petrous apicitis.

impression:
1. Normal temporal bone CT.
2. No middle ear effusion, mastoiditis, or inner ear abnormality identified.
author: RadReport Templates
